channel.title: TeslaBlog.eu — Tesla News & Updates
channel.description: Latest Tesla news, deals, and updates across Europe and beyond.

item.title: Tesla scales back driver monitoring with latest Full Self-Driving release
item.description: Tesla has scaled back driver monitoring to be less naggy with the latest version of the Full Self-Driving (Supervised) suite, which is version 14.3.3. The latest version is already earning praise from owners, who are reporting that the suite is far less invasive when it comes to keeping drivers f...

item.title: Tesla’s Texas Solar Megaplant Puts Energy Growth In Investor Focus
item.description: Tesla has begun full scale construction of a large solar panel manufacturing facility in Brookshire, Texas. The plant is being built alongside its Megapack Megafactory and is targeting 100 GW in annual solar capacity. This marks Tesla's largest push so far into vertically integrated solar and a m...

item.title: Tesla Expands FSD to Lithuania
item.description: The approval follows the Netherlands as Tesla looks for wider EU access.

item.title: Tesla can’t seem to figure out the power of its new cheap Model 3
item.description: Tesla has changed the 0-100 km/h acceleration spec on its new $39,490 CAD Model 3 Premium RWD in Canada for a second in less than three weeks. The car now lists a 6.2-second sprint — two full seconds slower than the 4.2 seconds Tesla advertised at launch on May 1. The repeated spec changes on the...

item.title: SpaceX IPO Could Rewrite Wall Street Records
item.description: Elon Musk's rocket company is reportedly targeting one of the biggest public listings ever.

item.title: Tesla Full Self-Driving expands in Europe, entering its second country
item.description: Tesla has officially expanded its Full Self-Driving (FSD) suite in Europe once again, as it will now be offered to customer vehicles in Lithuania, marking a significant milestone as the second European Union country to offer the system. Tesla confirmed FSD’s rollout in Lithuania this morning: FSD...

item.title: Tesla, Wipers, And The Malevolent Mr. Musk
item.description: Should a man who cannot make automatic windshield wipers work be trusted to make cars that drive themselves? The post Tesla, Wipers, And The Malevolent Mr. Musk appeared first on CleanTechnica .

item.title: A deep dive into SpaceX ahead of its Starship launch, S-1 filing
item.description: SpaceX's (SPAX.PVT) S-1 filing and upcoming Starship launch mark critical milestones for the company's anticipated initial public offering (IPO). Yahoo Finance's Pras Subramanian and Julie Hyman discuss the company's makeup.

item.title: Tesla launches FSD in second European country
item.description: Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” (Supervised) driver assistance software is now rolling out in Lithuania, making it the second European country to allow the system on public roads. The Lithuanian Transport Safety Administration confirmed it has recognized the Dutch RDW certification that first approve...

item.title: Tesla (TSLA) Slid Over Capex Concerns
item.description: Loomis Sayles, an investment management company, released its “Global Growth Fund” investor letter for the first quarter of 2026. A copy of the letter is available to download here. The fund reported -13.09% in the first quarter, lagging the MSCI ACWI Index Net’s -3.20% return.

item.title: Tesla Stock Rises. This Rival Wants to Fill Its Model S Void.
item.description: In January, Tesla announced plans to stop production of its high-end Model S and X vehicles, choosing to turn that manufacturing capacity in Fremont, California, into a line dedicated to humanoid robot production. Car production stopped in May. Tesla is delivering its final “Signature” versions...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Terafab Could Ultimately Cost $119 Billion — Why That Might Be Money Well Spent
item.description: Elon Musk’s visionary projects have been sparking some huge conversations in the AI world in recent months. Whether it’s the orbital AI data centers, which were given a massive vote of confidence following the recent Google (whose parent company is Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL)) deal with SpaceX, as we...

item.title: Why Boeing Has the Most to Lose If Tesla and SpaceX Ever Combine
item.description: Gene Munster of Deepwater Asset Management and Elon Musk biographer Walter Isaacson have floated the idea that Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) and SpaceX could combine within the next decade. This remains speculation rather than a deal. For Boeing (NYSE: BA), the hypothetical lands harder than for any other...

item.title: Tesla (TSLA) officially abandons India factory after years of broken promises
item.description: Tesla has officially confirmed it will not build a manufacturing facility in India, ending nearly a decade of negotiations, false starts, and broken promises with the Indian government. India’s Minister of Heavy Industries H.D. Kumaraswamy confirmed the decision on May 19, putting a definitive en...

item.title: Tesla launches Full Self-Driving in Lithuania
item.description: Tesla said on X on Wednesday ‌that its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) driver assistance ‌software was being rolled out in Lithuania, the ​second country in Europe to do so after Dutch approval last month. • Dutch regulator RDW is seeking EU-wide acceptance, but other member ‌states can ⁠recognise...

item.title: Jeff Bezos-Backed Slate Auto An 'Interesting Company To Watch,' Says Ross Gerber After Hanging Out With Employee That Helped Build Tesla
item.description: Investor Ross Gerber said on Sunday that a conversation with an early Tesla Inc. employee now working at Slate Auto led him to watch the electric vehicle startup more closely as it prepares to launch a low-cost pickup truck in...

item.title: Tesla Has Abandoned the Model S and Mercedes Wants to Fill the High-End EV Void
item.description: In January, Tesla announced plans to stop production of its high-end Model S and X vehicles, choosing to turn that manufacturing capacity in Fremont, California, into a line dedicated to humanoid robot production. Car production stopped in May. Tesla is delivering its final “Signature” versions...

item.title: Tesla FSD makes dangerous mistake in unfamiliar territory
item.description: Tesla Full Self-Driving (Supervised) finally made its way to Europe through the Netherlands last month as the first stop in its long-term plan to become legal throughout the European Union. But now that the tech is out in the wild, European drivers are running into some of the same issues U.S. FS...

item.title: The Global PFAS-Free Battery Market 2026-2036: Technologies, Regulation, and Forecasts - Profiles of 94 Companies Across Cells, Processes and Pack-level Systems Value Chain
item.description: The global PFAS-free battery market, driven by European regulations, US actions, and commitments from major automotive and consumer-electronics firms like Apple and Tesla, presents significant growth opportunities. With the European Commission set to adopt REACH restrictions by 2027 and increasin...

item.title: Tesla ramps up smart driving recruitment across 9 cities in China, fueling hopes for FSD approval
item.description: submitted by /u/Saurta17 [link] [comments]

item.title: Tesla ditches India after years of broken promises
item.description: Tesla’s long-running effort to establish a manufacturing presence in India is officially over. India’s Minister of Heavy Industries H.D. Kumaraswamy confirmed on May 19, 2026 that Tesla has informed authorities it will not proceed with a manufacturing facility in the country.

item.title: Tesla’s Former AI Lead Goes To Anthropic
item.description: Andrej Karpathy was Tesla’s AI lead well before AI was everywhere all the time. At the very early part of the AI hype cycle, he was the only AI guy many people knew. He led Tesla’s efforts toward Full Self Driving (FSD) for years, and it’s still not exactly clear ...

item.title: Elon promises FSD by year’s end, but Einride has L4 autonomy for big trucks TODAY
item.description: It’s a question of who is actually delivering product and who is just delivering promises on today’s delivery-heavy episode of Quick Charge ! Is the tenth time a charm for Elon’s FSD, or will Einride CEO Roozbeh Charli steal his self-driving thunder? more…

item.title: Volvo’s new EV costs the same to lease as a Tesla Model Y Premium, but here’s the catch
item.description: Volvo’s new “game-changing” midsize electric SUV, the 2027 EX60, is now on sale, but is it worth it to lease? more…

item.title: Anthropic Hires Former OpenAI Researcher Karpathy as AI Talent War Heats Up
item.description: Elon Musk once called Karpathy one of the top researchers in a specific area of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Nissan To Follow Tesla By Possibly Exporting EVs From China To Canada, Report
item.description: Nissan is considering using the same strategy as Tesla now that Canada has drastically lowered tariffs on Chinese-made electric vehicles. Meanwhile, Chinese EV makers are already angling to break into the Canadian market.

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck owner believed Elon Musk when he said it could cross a lake — now he’s in jail
item.description: A Tesla Cybertruck owner drove his electric pickup truck into Grapevine Lake in Texas to test the vehicle’s “Wade Mode” feature. The Cybertruck became disabled in the water and the driver was arrested. The incident is the latest in a growing list of Cybertruck owners taking Elon Musk’s claims abo...

item.title: This 100% electric flying car that jumped over a Tesla Cybertruck is coming soon
item.description: California-based startup Alef Aeronautics claims it’s the world’s first real electric flying car . After completing over 1,000 flights, including hopping over a Tesla Cybertruck, Alef is preparing to begin customer deliveries later this year. more…

item.title: Tesla drops as mounting headwinds test lofty valuation
item.description: Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares fell more than 3% on Tuesday, pressured by a combination of legal setbacks, a price increase on its best-selling model and growing concern that an imminent SpaceX listing will draw investor capital away from the electric vehicle maker. Much of the selling...

item.title: Ex-Tesla AI Director Joins Anthropic: How Andrej Karpathy Switched Sides
item.description: Andrej Karpathy, one of OpenAI’s founding members and the former director of AI at Tesla Inc (NASDAQ:TSLA), announced Tuesday morning that he is joining Anthropic. “I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative,” Karpathy wrote, adding that he plans to return to ...

item.title: What the Musk–Altman legal battle means for OpenAI, SpaceX IPOs
item.description: OpenAI's (OPAI.PVT) Sam Altman was not found liable for Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk's claims, due to clear-cut statute-of-limitations issues. Yahoo Finance's Julie Hyman and Jake Conley discuss the impacts on OpenAI's and SpaceX's (SPAX.PVT) upcoming initial public offerings (IPOs).

item.title: Tesla Hikes Model Y Prices as Wall Street Focuses on AI. Whether You Are Betting on Cars or Robots, TSLA Stock Is Overstretched Here.
item.description: Tesla hikes its Model Y prices for the first time in two years.

item.title: SpaceX’s IPO to Mint Millionaires in Poor Texas Border Town
item.description: (Bloomberg) -- A 45-minute drive down a narrow highway separates one of America’s poorest border cities from the heart of Elon Musk’s SpaceX empire.Most Read from BloombergUS Lawmakers Plan New $130 Fee for Electric Vehicle OwnersBillionaire Rinehart Bets $100 Million on US Defense StocksTrump Sa...

item.title: Tesla Faces Valuation Test as Energy Unit Expands
item.description: Strong margins and long-term demand are drawing attention, but valuation remains high.

item.title: SpaceX reveals date for maiden Starship v3 launch
item.description: SpaceX has revealed the date for the maiden voyage of Starship v3, its newest and most advanced version of the rocket yet. Starship v3 represents a significant leap forward. At 124 meters tall when fully stacked, it stands taller than previous versions and boasts substantial upgrades .

item.title: Exclusive: Tesla (TSLA) is building its giant solar panel factory in Houston
item.description: Electrek has learned that Tesla (TSLA) is building its massive new solar panel manufacturing operation at its facility in Brookshire, Texas, near Houston. The factory will be co-located with the Megapack Megafactory Tesla is already constructing at the same site. A source familiar with the plans ...

item.title: MetaMask Just Added Tokenized US Stocks – NOT for US Users
item.description: On February 3, 2026, MetaMask announced an integration with Ondo Finance that lets eligible users buy, hold, and trade tokenized US stocks, ETFs, and commodities, including Tesla, NVIDIA, Apple, and gold, directly in their self-custodial wallets. The real question isn’t whether this is interestin...

item.title: Forget Tesla. The Robotics Company Actually Shipping Revenue Has a $22 Billion Backlog and Nobody Is Talking About It
item.description: Everyone is still glued to Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) because a Q1 earnings beat, the robotaxi pitch, and the Optimus humanoid tease have convinced retail traders the autonomy story finally pays off this year. The Tesla Trade Is Crowded and Priced for a Miracle Tesla carries a P/E of 406 and a free cash...

item.title: Tesla: Why It Refuses To Crash No Matter What I Think (Upgrade)
Elon Musk breaks silence on OpenAI trial decision
item.description: Elon Musk broke his silence regarding the jury decision to throw out the case against OpenAI and Sam Altman. The Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI frontman has already indicated that an appeal will be filed regarding the decision, which went against him yesterday. A Federal jury dismissed this high-profile ...

item.title: OpenAI Defeats Tesla CEO Elon Musk's Lawsuit
item.description: The ruling removes a major legal obstacle as OpenAI explores future growth plans.

item.title: Tesla vs. BYD: Which Stock Will Outperform The Market In 2026
item.description: Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) and BYD (OTC: BYDDF) sit on opposite sides of the global EV map. Tesla just posted a Q1 2026 beat with $0.41 EPS versus $0.35 expected, snapping a rough 2025. BYD continues to print record monthly EV and PHEV volumes out of China.

item.title: 3 Reasons to Sell TSLA and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
item.description: Tesla has followed the market’s trajectory closely, rising in tandem with the S&P 500 over the past six months. The stock has climbed by 8.2% to $442.40 per share while the index has gained 11.5%.

item.title: Is Trending Stock Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) a Buy Now?
item.description: Tesla (TSLA) has been one of the stocks most watched by Zacks.com users lately. So, it is worth exploring what lies ahead for the stock.

item.title: If You Invested $10,000 In Tesla 10 Years Ago, This Is How Rich You Would Be Today
item.description: Among “The Magnificent Seven” stocks that have been buoying the Dow Jones Industrial Average to new heights—maintaining its status largely thanks to maturing software-as-a-service models and roughly 1.28 million active Full Self-Driving (FSD) subscribers—none of those companies’ founders eclipses...

item.title: Tesla Hikes Prices, and the Stock Takes a Hit
item.description: Investors didn’t seem to like Tesla raising prices of its popular Model Y vehicles in the U.S. over the weekend. Shares of the electric vehicle maker were down 0.9% at $406.25 in premarket trading on Tuesday, while and futures were down 0.3% and 0.2%, respectively.

item.title: Tesla Robotaxi Plans Face U.S. Expansion Test
item.description: Robotaxi growth remains central to Tesla's outlook as vehicle sales slow.

item.title: Global Commercial Humanoid Robotics Market Research 2025-2030 with Focus on China and United States Featuring Figure AI, Agility, Tesla, UBTECH, Boston Dynamics, Apptronik, AgiBot, Unitree, and Leju
item.description: Key market opportunities lie in the growing adoption of humanoid robotics in industrial logistics, a focus on bridging investment and revenue disparities, and leveraging China's capacity growth strategy. Companies achieving commercialization, like UBTECH and Agility, highlight paths to market mat...

item.title: SpaceX Is Kicking Off IPO Mania -- but It's Also Reviving Stock-Split Euphoria
item.description: Statistically, since 1980, companies that have conducted forward stock splits have handily outperformed the benchmark S&P 500 in the 12 months following their split announcement.

item.title: Stock Markets Now Shrug at Trump’s Iran Rhetoric. That’s a Big Worry.
item.description: Musk vows to appeal OpenAI verdict, House lawmakers water down housing affordability bill, the world needs more rare earth mines, and more news to start your day.
